// storyboard · screen recording + voiceover

Massive Web Render —
Geotargeted Response Comparison

Neutral narrator. Real terminal. One parameter change drives the whole demo. Webcam on throughout.

FormatMP4 · 1080×720
Duration~100 seconds
AudioNeutral narrator + ambient
StyleScreen Recording + Voiceover + Webcam
PlacementFeature demo · Social / Blog embed
# Frame Visual Scene Name Timestamp Screen Content Voiceover Description
01
REC
🧑‍💻
Narrator
Massive Web Render
Webcam only
Narrator intro. No screen. Sets the premise.
0:00 – 0:10
Intro
📺 Screen
No screen — webcam only
🎙 Voiceover
"AI models don't give the same answer everywhere. Prices, policies, and local context all change what you get back. I'm going to show you exactly what that looks like — one prompt, two countries, two completely different responses."
Opens on webcam only. No screen share yet. Narrator speaks directly to camera — casual, not salesy. Establishes the hook before any demo begins. Hold for a beat after the last word, then transition to screen.
02
docs.joinmassive.com/web-render
// massive web render
Query any AI model.
From any country.
ChatGPT Gemini Perplexity
🧑‍💻
Narrator · Massive
Screen + webcam
Docs page. Narrator walks through what the API does.
0:10 – 0:20
Context
📺 Screen
Docs page visible. Cursor moves slowly across the supported models. No typing yet.
🎙 Voiceover
"Massive Web Render routes your queries through a residential network — so when you ask ChatGPT a question, it's coming from a real IP in that country, not a datacenter. Let me show you a live example."
Screen share begins. Webcam moves to PIP in the corner — stays there for the rest of the demo. Docs page is static while narrator provides context. Cursor drifts slowly over the model pills.
03
terminal
$ curl -X POST api.joinmassive.com/render \
-H "Authorization: Bearer $KEY" \
-d '{
"model": "chatgpt",
"prompt": "Best EV to buy?",
"country": "US"
}'
# running...
🧑‍💻
Narrator · Massive
Screen + webcam
Terminal. First call typed live — routed through US.
0:20 – 0:35
First call
📺 Screen
Cursor types the curl command live. Enter pressed. Response streams in — ChatGPT recommends Tesla Model 3, mentions US federal tax credit. Narrator highlights the tax credit line.
🎙 Voiceover
"Here's a simple call — we're asking ChatGPT for the best EV to buy, routing through a US residential IP. The response comes back in under two seconds."
Switches to terminal. Command typed live as narrator walks through each parameter. Enter pressed. Response streams in. Narrator reads the key phrase aloud. Webcam PIP stays bottom-right.
04
terminal
$ curl -X POST api.joinmassive.com/render \
-H "Authorization: Bearer $KEY" \
-d '{
"model": "chatgpt",
"prompt": "Best EV to buy?",
"country": "DE" ← only change
}'
🧑‍💻
Narrator · Massive
Screen + webcam
Same command. One change: "US" → "DE".
0:35 – 0:50
Second call
📺 Screen
Cursor highlights "US", deletes, types "DE" deliberately. Red highlight appears on the country value. Enter pressed. Response streams in.
🎙 Voiceover
"Now the exact same prompt — one change. We swap the country to Germany. That's it. Same model, same question, different routing."
Command recalled from history. Cursor moves to "US", deletes, types "DE" slowly — deliberately paced so viewers track what changed. Enter pressed. Response streams in.
05
🇺🇸
ChatGPT · US
Tesla Model 3
mentions $7,500
federal tax credit
🇩🇪
ChatGPT · DE
Volkswagen ID.4
≠ different
🧑‍💻
Narrator · Massive
Screen + webcam
Split view. Two responses side by side.
0:50 – 1:08
The comparison
📺 Screen
Both responses side by side. Cursor hovers tax credit mention on the left, then subsidy mention on the right. Subtle highlight on each as narrator calls them out.
🎙 Voiceover
"The US response recommends a Tesla and mentions the federal tax credit. The German response recommends a Volkswagen ID.4 and references local subsidies. Same model. Same prompt. Completely different answers — because the internet it's seeing is different."
Comparison layout — US left, DE right. Cursor hovers the tax credit mention, then the subsidy mention. This is the core "wow" moment. Webcam PIP visible bottom-right throughout.
06
REC
🧑‍💻
Narrator
Massive Web Render
Webcam only
Narrator reacts. Drives the insight home before moving on.
1:08 – 1:16
Reaction beat
📺 Screen
No screen — webcam only
🎙 Voiceover
"That's not a fluke. That's how geo-aware AI responses work. And you can do this across every country Massive supports."
Screen share drops. Full webcam for a beat. Narrator looks directly at camera — lands the takeaway before moving to scale. Short but important: gives the viewer a moment to absorb the comparison.
07
terminal — 5 country loop
# loop across 5 countries
for c in US DE JP BR IN; do
query --country $c --prompt "Best EV?"
done
✓ US → Tesla Model 3
✓ DE → Volkswagen ID.4
✓ JP → Nissan Ariya
▌ BR running...
🧑‍💻
Narrator · Massive
Screen + webcam
Loop across 5 countries. Results print fast.
1:16 – 1:32
Scale
📺 Screen
Loop runs. US, DE, JP, BR, IN results print one by one — each a different recommendation. Counter overlay: "5 / 195 countries." Fast but readable.
🎙 Voiceover
"One loop, one API key — 195 countries. You're seeing the internet the way real users in every market see it."
Back to screen share. Loop runs — results print sequentially, each a different car recommendation. Counter overlay visible. The point: this isn't a two-country trick, it scales everywhere.
08
docs.joinmassive.com/web-render
// get started
Try Massive Web Render →
docs.joinmassive.com/web-render
Free tier · No credit card required
🧑‍💻
Narrator · Massive
Screen + webcam
Back to docs. URL on screen. No hard sell.
1:32 – 1:42
CTA
📺 Screen
Cursor rests on the docs URL. Page static. URL stays visible for a full 5 seconds. Fade out.
🎙 Voiceover
"Massive Web Render is live now. Link in the description — free tier, no credit card required."
Returns to docs. Cursor rests on the URL. One calm closing sentence. URL holds on screen for 5 full seconds before fade. Webcam PIP stays visible to the end — human sign-off.
Voiceover line
Screen action
Pure webcam scene
Ambient audio — no music
Live terminal output
